exemplar.title: "How do geographical location (urban vs. rural) and the cost of resources affect the accessibility and usage of contraceptive methods in Kenya?â

detailedExemplarData.title: "How do geographical location (urban vs. rural) and the cost of resources affect the accessibility and usage of contraceptive methods in Kenya?â

strengths: The topic is communicated clearly and consistently throughout, with precise framing around urbanârural contrasts and contraception access in Kenya.
The research question is stated verbatim and remains fully focused, anchoring each analytical section directly to its variables and outcomes.
Signposting is effective (table of contents, section headings) and links to the World Studies theme are well maintained, aiding reader navigation.
weaknesses: Methodology justification is only partially developed; there is little discussion of sampling limitations, ethical considerations, or basic statistical treatments.
Primary data are limited to a single convenience interview conducted outside the Kenyan context, reducing methodological robustness.
Ancillary factors (education, climate) are discussed but not systematically integrated into the chosen methods.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (topic communication and research question) clearly meet the 5â6 band descriptors, while methodology justification fits the 3â4 band. Balancing these, a final Focus and Method score of 5 out of 6 accurately reflects the overall high clarity and focus, tempered by methodological gaps.

strengths: A substantial range of authoritative, relevant sources (KDHS, WHO, peer-reviewed articles, policy documents) is employed, demonstrating discernment in selection.
Disciplinary knowledge is clear and coherent, with biological mechanisms and geographical concepts applied accurately.
Specialist terminology is used with appropriate depth and consistency, enhancing the academic tone.
weaknesses: A minority of semi-popular websites (Healthline, MedicalNewsToday) and occasional broken or duplicated URLs reduce overall academic rigor.
Some explanatory passages become overly descriptive without always linking back to the research question.
Several data points and policy claims lack precise in-text citations, weakening source verifiability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Source selection and knowledge application consistently meet the 3â4 band, with terminology occasionally reaching the 5â6 band. Overall, a score of 4 out of 6 reflects coherent understanding tempered by source-reliability and integration issues.

strengths: Research draws on a broad mix of credible quantitative and qualitative sources, demonstrating deliberate and relevant selection.
Analysis integrates biological, geographical, and economic evidence into a coherent interdisciplinary argument with well-supported sectional conclusions.
Argument structure is logical and methodical, with clear mini-conclusions and a synthesis that addresses the central question in depth.
weaknesses: Some analytical leaps (e.g., the London employment analogy) are insufficiently justified and risk overgeneralisation.
Statistical rigor is limited by the absence of significance testing or detailed reliability checks.
The final conclusion restates findings more than offering new synthesis or forward-looking recommendations.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands sit firmly in the 7â9 band, with strong interdisciplinary insight and mostly well-supported conclusions. The work just misses top-band rigour, so a high-end 9 out of 12 accurately captures its critical-thinking performance.

strengths: The investigation follows a clear academic structure with an effective table of contents, logical sectioning, and properly placed research question and word count.
Headings and transitions facilitate coherent navigation through introduction, methodology, analysis, and conclusion.
Essential formatting conventions (title page, section numbering) are consistently applied.
weaknesses: Visual elements lack consistent numbering and formal captions; several figures are pixelated or redundant.
Citation style fluctuates between authorâdate, numbered footnotes, and web titles; bibliography entries are inconsistently formatted and duplicated.
Some key data (e.g., maps, tables) rely on alt-text or appendices rather than clear in-text presentation, reducing immediate clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Academic structure clearly meets the 3â4 band, but inconsistent visuals and referencing limit the presentation score to 3 out of 4, reflecting a generally coherent format with notable formatting weaknesses.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The essay follows a clear argumentative sequence, integrating biological explanations with geographic and economic evidence to build a multifaceted case. Each thematic section culminates in mini-conclusions that feed the final synthesis, and contrasts between Nairobi and Mandera are consistently drawn.  
There is some critical evaluationâe.g., acknowledging religious and traditional counter-arguments and data limitationsâbut depth of evaluation varies; reliability assessments are asserted rather than demonstrated. Occasional leaps in logic (e.g., extrapolating London employment data to Kenyan health-service funding) reveal moderate, not comprehensive, critical scrutiny.  
The argument is largely logical and interdisciplinary with flashes of nuance, fitting the 7â9 band at a score of 7.

In-text citations are present, but styles fluctuate between author-date, numbered footnotes, and miscellaneous web titles; several URLs are duplicated or broken. The bibliography is extensive yet contains repeated entries and inconsistent formatting (capitalisation, italics, line breaks).  
Basic referencing requirements are metâsources are cited and a bibliography is suppliedâbut the lack of systematic formatting and occasional omissions (e.g., missing publication details) prevent attainment of the top band. Hence the work fits the 1â2 band with a score of 2.

The student states the topic explicitly in the title, restates and explains it in the opening paragraph, and maintains a consistent urbanârural/cost-of-resources framing throughout the essay. Key concepts such as âaccessibilityâ and âusageâ are defined, and the Kenyan focus is reiterated in each major section and the conclusion, ensuring sustained clarity for the reader.  
â¢  Coherent signposting (table of contents, section headings) and repeated links to the World-Studies theme of Health & Development demonstrate an appropriate communication style for an EE.  
â¢  Minor digressions into climate and education broaden the scope slightly, but these are acknowledged as ancillary factors and do not obscure the central topic. Overall, the level of clarity and consistency meets the descriptor for the 5â6 band.

The essay meets academic conventions: a detailed table of contents, numbered sub-sections, introductionâmethodologyâanalysisâconclusion flow, and correctly placed RQ and word count demonstrate a coherent structure. Transitions between sections are generally logical, allowing readers to follow the development of ideas.  
Minor issuesâsuch as duplicate RQ listingâdo not disrupt overall clarity. The structure therefore aligns with the 3â4 band; the stronger end (4) is justified by clear internal organisation and effective signposting.

The essay demonstrates clear disciplinary knowledge: biological mechanisms of contraceptive methods are explained accurately (hormonal feedback, IUD function), and geographical concepts such as spatial distribution and socio-economic disparity are applied.  
Source material is usually integrated through data tables, graphs, and discussion, supporting claims about cost and prevalence. However, some explanatory sections become descriptive (e.g., lengthy summaries of side-effects) without always linking back to the RQ, and a few inferences (climate impact, London employment analogy) lack sufficient evidence.  
Overall, the knowledge is generally coherent and relevant, but integration is uneven, aligning best with the upper end of the 3â4 band (score 4).

The analysis is generally coherent and moves logically from distribution to usage, cost, and sociocultural factors, demonstrating synthesis of biological and geographical evidence. Quantitative data are transformed into tables, graphs, and cost-to-income ratios, and these are interpreted to support the central argument that urban areas enjoy better contraceptive access and uptake.  
Key conclusionsâe.g., cost burdens in Mandera, influence of facility distribution, and role of religionâare mostly grounded in cited data. The student also recognises limitations and alternative factors (education, climate), adding nuance.  
However, occasional assertions (London employment analogy, climateâtransport link) lack direct evidence, and statistical rigor (significance testing) is absent, preventing a move to the highest band. The work aligns with the mid-range of the 7â9 band, meriting a score of 8.

The research question is presented verbatim on the title page and in the introduction; it is concise, uses clear variables (geographical location, cost of resources) and outcomes (accessibility and usage of contraception), and is appropriately narrow for 4 000 words.  
The essay remains firmly anchored to this question, with each analytical section (distribution, usage, cost) mapping directly onto an element of the RQ. Occasional mention of climate/education is framed as supplementary, preserving focus.  
Consequently, the question is both clearly stated and fully focused, justifying placement in the 5â6 band.

The majority of the researchânational DHS micro-data, WHO factsheets, Kenyan policy documents, and price statistics from recognised NGOsâis clearly relevant and well matched to the question, showing deliberate selection from credible, varied disciplines.  
There is evidence of cross-checking and basic source evaluation in the methodology review, though limited detail is provided. The inclusion of one primary interview broadens perspective, albeit with acknowledged limitations.  
The presence of some popular-science websites slightly weakens overall rigor, so the strand does not reach the 10â12 band, but the breadth, pertinence, and mostly authoritative nature of sources justify the lower end of the 7â9 band (score 7).

A mixed-methods approach (KDHS statistics, WHO data, policy documents, and a primary interview) is outlined and generally appropriate to the RQ, showing informed selection of quantitative and qualitative evidence. The student explains how secondary data will be used for mapping/graphing and how the interview provides frontline insight.  
However, primary research is limited to a single convenience interview conducted outside the Kenyan context, and there is no discussion of sampling limitations or triangulation procedures beyond brief assertions. Methodological justification for data reliability checks and statistical treatment (e.g., significance testing) is absent.  
These gaps indicate that, while the chosen methods are suitable, the justification is only partially developed, meriting the 3â4 band with a score of 4.

The bibliography contains a substantial range of authoritative materialsâKDHS, WHO, peer-reviewed journals, government policy documentsâdemonstrating mostly relevant and appropriate selection. The student also incorporates maps, cost data from NGOs, and an original interview, showing an attempt at triangulation.  
Nevertheless, a noticeable minority of sources (e.g., Healthline, MedicalNewsToday, Gynopedia) are semi-popular and reduce overall academic rigor; duplication and broken URLs further weaken perceived reliability.  
Given the predominance of pertinent, scholarly sources but with some inconsistencies, the work fits securely in the 3â4 band, scored at 4.

The student includes multiple maps, tables, and charts that are relevant to the argument and mostly self-produced. Captions are provided, but several visuals are pixelated, lack consistent numbering (Figure 3 is a table, Figure 4 repeats information), and some legends or data sources are embedded in alt-text rather than formal captions.  
Although the visuals aid comprehension, inconsistent formatting and occasional redundancy limit their effectiveness, situating performance within the 1â2 band (awarded 2).

Throughout the investigation the student employs specialist terminologyâCPR, IUD, vasectomy, supply chain, contraceptive prevalence, follicle-stimulating hormoneâaccurately and consistently. Definitions are provided where needed, and biological mechanisms are articulated with appropriate precision for IB level.  
Minor slips (e.g., âMaderaâ for Mandera, typographical errors) do not undermine conceptual accuracy. The student demonstrates a solid grasp of both biology and geography concepts, integrating them appropriately in an interdisciplinary manner.  
The depth and correctness of terminology use place this strand in the 5â6 band; a score of 5 is awarded to acknowledge slight inconsistencies without unduly penalizing overall command.

annotations.comment: Typographical error: âMaderaâ should be âManderaâ to maintain consistency in place-name usage.
Claims about Kenyan Vision 2030, the 2010 Constitution, and the Health Act must be supported by precise citations to validate these legal and policy references.
While including education and climate acknowledges complexity, ensure these factors are methodologically integrated and explicitly tied back to the research question.
The conclusion restates findings but adds little new synthesis; include specific recommendations or implications directly based on your analysis.
The student has provided the word count at the beginning of the report, satisfying the formatting requirement.
Methodological justification is superficial; discuss sampling limitations, data reliability checks and consider basic statistical treatments to strengthen your mixed-methods approach.
Figure 2 map is relevant but lacks consistent numbering and formal caption formatting; unify figure numbering and standardize caption style.
The London employment analogy is insufficiently justified in the Kenyan context; either provide stronger evidence for transferability or remove to avoid overgeneralisation.
Define the abbreviation âCPRâ at first use in-text rather than relying solely on a footnote for improved clarity.
The percentage values in Figure 4 do not precisely match the source data (e.g., Female Sterilization ~2% vs 3.30%); ensure graphical values accurately reflect the underlying table.
The title of the investigation is clearly stated at the start, meeting the requirement for title placement.
The detailed table of contents effectively signposts the structure and enhances the readerâs ability to navigate the essay.
In-text citation styles fluctuate (e.g., author-date vs. web titles); adopt a consistent citation format throughout to improve referencing clarity.
The SDG Globe graphic is conceptually relevant but lacks a clear link to Kenya; consider using Kenya-specific visuals to strengthen your contextual analysis.
Introduce the cost-as-percentage-of-income formula and its key results in the main text rather than deferring entirely to the appendix.
The facility count statistics for Nairobi and Mandera require precise source citation (author, year, dataset) to verify data accuracy.
Figure 3 is presented as a table; converting it into a chart would facilitate easier comparison and enhance visual communication.
The biological mechanism of the pill is accurately described but needs explicit linkage back to Kenyan accessibility and usage to maintain focus on the RQ.
The statistic that â30% of women say distance is a major challengeâ should be accompanied by full study details (author, year) rather than just a URL.
The description of cost increases by 20â30% is descriptive; critically analyze how this differential concretely affects usage rates in rural communities.
Clarify the year and context for the manufacturing cost figures (e.g., Ksh 3,800â32,000) and cite the precise source.
Extend the cost-income percentage analysis to additional contraceptive methods (e.g., condoms, pills) for a more comprehensive assessment of financial burden.
The claim that IUD usage is lower in developing countries requires citation from recent DHS data or peer-reviewed literature to substantiate it.
Ensure all sentences are complete; the description of the IUD as a âT-shaped plastic structure that isâ is left unfinished.
The discussion of religious views on permanent methods requires engagement with opposing studies for a more nuanced, balanced evaluation.
Summarize primary interview insights in the methodology review and move detailed transcripts to the appendix to streamline the main narrative.
The comparison of fertility rates (2.8 vs 3.9) should be accompanied by a precise citation (source title, year) to reinforce data credibility.
Ensure URLs are not split across lines and are presented with correct hyperlink formatting to maintain clarity in references.
Figure 6 lacks a legend or direct labels to indicate which bar corresponds to Nairobi vs Mandera; include clear labeling in the chart area.
Claims about climate and education influencing access lack supporting data; integrate relevant statistics or studies to substantiate these assertions.
Consider ending the conclusion with a clear, forward-looking recommendation that directly addresses the research question for stronger closure.
Your methodology review acknowledges strengths and limitations but omits discussion of ethical considerations and sampling constraints for the primary interview.
The source evaluation notes reliability but does not critically assess the academic rigor of popular websites (e.g., Healthline); reflect on how this affects overall research quality.
Bibliography entries exhibit inconsistent formatting and duplication; adopt a standardized citation style and remove repeats for improved presentation.
